# radosgw-admin

Node.js SDK for the Ceph RADOS Gateway (RGW) Admin Ops REST API. Manage users, buckets, quotas, rate limits and access keys programmatically from Node.js.

## When to use this
- You have a Ceph cluster (Rook-Ceph, OpenShift Data Foundation, bare metal) and need to automate admin tasks from Node.js
- You want to provision/deprovision S3 users, enforce quotas, or manage bucket ownership via code
- There was no actively maintained Node.js SDK for the RGW Admin Ops API and you need one
- You are building Kubernetes operators, automation scripts, or admin tooling on top of Ceph RGW

## Install
- npm: `npm install radosgw-admin`

## Entry Point
- `RadosGWAdminClient` from package root.

## Quick Start
```ts
import { RadosGWAdminClient } from 'radosgw-admin';

const rgw = new RadosGWAdminClient({
  host: 'https://ceph-rgw.example.com',
  accessKey: process.env.RGW_ACCESS_KEY,
  secretKey: process.env.RGW_SECRET_KEY,
});

// Create a user and set a 10 GB quota
await rgw.users.create({ uid: 'alice', displayName: 'Alice' });
await rgw.quota.setUserQuota({ uid: 'alice', maxSize: '10G', enabled: true });

// Transfer a bucket to another user
await rgw.buckets.transferOwnership({ bucket: 'my-bucket', uid: 'alice' });
```

## Modules
- `users`: create/get/getByAccessKey/modify/delete/list/suspend/enable/getStats
- `keys`: generate/revoke
- `subusers`: create/modify/remove
- `buckets`: list/getInfo/delete/transferOwnership/removeOwnership/verifyIndex
- `quota`: user and bucket quota get/set/enable/disable operations
- `rateLimit`: user, bucket, and global rate-limit operations
- `usage`: get/trim usage logs
- `info`: get cluster info (FSID, backends)

## Compatibility
- Node.js >= 18, Bun
- Ceph Pacific, Reef, Squid and later
- Rook-Ceph, OpenShift Data Foundation (ODF), bare-metal Ceph

## Error Classes
- `RGWError`
- `RGWValidationError`
- `RGWAuthError`
- `RGWNotFoundError`
- `RGWConflictError`

## HTTP Error Mapping
- 400 -> `RGWValidationError`
- 403 -> `RGWAuthError`
- 404 -> `RGWNotFoundError`
- 409 -> `RGWConflictError`
- 5xx -> `RGWError`

## Safety Notes
- Destructive operations (`purgeData`, `purgeObjects`, `removeAll`) permanently delete data — use only with explicit intent.
- `insecure: true` disables TLS verification and is not recommended for production.

## Canonical References
- Website & Docs: https://nycanshu.github.io/radosgw-admin/
- Repo README: ./README.md
- Full machine-readable reference: ./llms-full.txt
- Ceph RGW Admin Ops API: https://docs.ceph.com/en/latest/radosgw/adminops/
